explanation: This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the STANDBY state.  It uses the until operator (U) to express the conditional transition. The property asserts that if standby is true, then it will remain true until the autopilot changes from NOMINAL to STANDBY.

This LTL formula captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the STANDBY state.  It uses the until operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot must remain in the NOMINAL state until the STANDBY state is reached. The implication (->) ensures that this transition occurs only when standby is true.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the pilot is in control (standby is true), the autopilot will eventually transition from the NOMINAL state to the STANDBY state.  It uses the "until" operator (U) to express the condition that the autopilot remains in the NOMINAL state until the STANDBY state is reached. The property does not specify the exact timing or sequence of events during the transition; it only asserts that the transition will happen eventually once the condition is met.

This LTL property states that, globally, if the system is in the NOMINAL state and the pilot is in control (standby), then in the next state, the system will be in the STANDBY state.  It directly captures the requirement that the autopilot transitions from NOMINAL to STANDBY when the pilot takes control.

The property ensures that if standby is true at any point, eventually the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state and previously in the NOMINAL state. It does not specify the exact order of events or time constraints.

The use of G ensures this property holds globally throughout the operation.

The formula G (standby -> F state_is_STANDBY) ensures that whenever the pilot is in control, eventually the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state. The additional conjunct G (state_is_NOMINAL & standby -> X F state_is_STANDBY) ensures that the change of state happens in the next step and the condition of the pilot being in control must be true when the autopilot is in NOMINAL state.
output_LTL: G (standby -> F state_is_STANDBY) & G (state_is_NOMINAL & standby -> X F state_is_STANDBY)
